title: Example gallery
text: examples page use -memory DuckDB database loaded data. Multiple datasets used page organized dataset analyzed.
code: library(rsgl) #>  #> Attaching package: 'rsgl' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:datasets': #>  #>     cars, trees library(duckdb) #> Loading required package: DBI  con <- dbConnect(duckdb())

title: Example gallery
code: dbWriteTable(con, "cars", cars) dbGetQuery(con, "   select *   from cars   limit 5 ") #>   car_id horsepower miles_per_gallon origin year #> 1      1        130               18    USA 1970 #> 2      2        165               15    USA 1970 #> 3      3        150               18    USA 1970 #> 4      4        150               16    USA 1970 #> 5      5        140               17    USA 1970

title: Example gallery
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(miles_per_gallon) as x,     count(*) as y   from cars   group by     bin(miles_per_gallon)   using bars ")

title: Example gallery
code: dbWriteTable(con, "trees", trees) dbGetQuery(con, "   select *   from trees   limit 5 ") #>   tree_id  age circumference #> 1       1  118            30 #> 2       1  484            58 #> 3       1  664            87 #> 4       1 1004           115 #> 5       1 1231           120

title: Example gallery
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     age as x,     circumference as y   from trees   collect by     tree_id   using lines ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     age as theta,     radius as r   from (     select       *,       circumference/(2*pi()) as radius     from trees   )   collect by     tree_id   using lines ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: use examples tutorial, create -memory DuckDB database load two tables, cars trees. Let’s query view sample data:
code: library(rsgl) #>  #> Attaching package: 'rsgl' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:datasets': #>  #>     cars, trees library(duckdb) #> Loading required package: DBI  con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", cars) dbWriteTable(con, "trees", trees) dbGetQuery(con, "   select *   from cars   limit 5 ") #>   car_id horsepower miles_per_gallon origin year #> 1      1        130               18    USA 1970 #> 2      2        165               15    USA 1970 #> 3      3        150               18    USA 1970 #> 4      4        150               16    USA 1970 #> 5      5        140               17    USA 1970 dbGetQuery(con, "   select *   from trees   limit 5 ") #>   tree_id  age circumference #> 1       1  118            30 #> 2       1  484            58 #> 3       1  664            87 #> 4       1 1004           115 #> 5       1 1231           120

title: Get started with rsgl
text: primary interface rsgl dbGetPlot function, takes DBI database connection SGL statement returns corresponding plot. Although examples tutorial use connection DuckDB database, dbGetPlot accept DBI connection.

title: Get started with rsgl
text: keyword precedes data source specification, often name table database. , specify cars table data source.  similar usage keyword SQL, except single data source allowed (.e., comma-separated list table names valid). data multiple sources pre-processing data necessary, SQL subquery can provided:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from (     select *     from cars     where origin = 'Japan'   )   using points ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: using keyword precedes name geometric object(s) represent data. Following ggplot2 terminology, geometric objects referred geoms. previous examples demonstrated representing data point geoms.

title: Get started with rsgl
text: visualize keyword precedes aesthetic--column mapping, maps perceivable traits geoms data source columns. prior examples mapped x y positions point geoms data source columns. However, aesthetics may non-positional, shown . visualize keyword closely resembles select keyword within SQL.  addition mapping aesthetics columns, aesthetics can mapped expressions include transformations aggregations.
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y,     origin as color   from cars   using points ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: SGL supports column-level transformations aggregations, shown binning transformation combined count aggregation produce histogram miles_per_gallon.  see , similarly SQL, SGL group clause aggregation groupings specified. Although SQL supports column-level transformation, grouping, aggregation, desirable SGL provide additional support operations. Statistical graphics often require operations binning natively supported SQL. Additionally, SGL’s column-level transformations aggregations performed scaling, easily replicated using SQL. example feature, binning counting applied log scaling, resulting log-scaled histogram.
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(miles_per_gallon) as x,     count(*) as y   from cars   group by     bin(miles_per_gallon)   using bars ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(miles_per_gallon) as x,     count(*) as y   from cars   group by     bin(miles_per_gallon)   using bars   scale by     log(x) ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: SGL, geom classified individual represents record (transformation aggregation) distinct geometric object. Alternatively, geom classified collective represents multiple records one geometric object. example, points lines individual collective geoms, respectively, shown data represented using .   collective geoms, collection records represent object determined automatically using reasonable defaults. behavior can overridden providing explicit collections collect clause. collect clause similar group clause, except rather defining groups aggregate , collect clause defines collections records represented one object. see example default collection ideal, followed preferrable explicit collection.
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     year as x,     avg(miles_per_gallon) as y   from cars   group by     year   using points ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     year as x,     avg(miles_per_gallon) as y   from cars   group by     year   using line ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     age as x,     circumference as y   from trees   using line ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     age as x,     circumference as y   from trees   collect by     tree_id   using lines ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: Geom qualifiers modify geoms positionally represent data, specified keywords precede geom names within using clause. Geom qualifiers can largely classified two groups, statistical qualifiers collision qualifiers. Statistical qualifiers modify positional representation via statistical transformation, linear regression:  Collision qualifiers specify positional adjustments relevant overlapping objects. see example using jittered qualifier add small amount random variation overlapping points discernible. Without jittered qualifier:  jittered qualifier:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     age as x,     circumference as y   from trees   using regression line ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     origin as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     origin as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using jittered points ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: graphics previous sections contain single layer geometric objects. However, common use multiple layers objects represent data single graphic. SGL, multiple layers can combined using layer operator. example, can layer regression line scatterplot:  Layers often share data source aesthetic mapping. reduce verbosity cases, layer operator can applied directly geom expressions:  Layers may different data sources aesthetic mappings. However, graphic single scale aesthetic, regardless number layers. result, given aesthetic must mapped consistent type across layers present, e.g. aesthetic mapped numerical type one layer categorical type another.
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points    layer    visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using regression line ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using (     points     layer     regression line   ) ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: mapped aesthetic scale determines data values mapped corresponding visual property. Scales determined implicitly default, can explicitly specified within scale clause. specify log scale x y aesthetics, overriding default linear scaling numerical mappings:  Scaling performed prior column-level transformations aggregations. Additionally, scaling performed prior positional modifications specified geom qualifiers, e.g., regression calculation performed log-scaling x y aesthetics. Scaling functions log applied aesthetic names rather column names modifications aesthetic scales rather actual data values. distinction demonstrated , log function instead applied actual data values SQL subquery:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using (     points     layer     regression line   )   scale by     log(x), log(y) ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     log_hp as x,     log_mpg as y   from (     select       log(horsepower) as log_hp,       log(miles_per_gallon) as log_mpg     from cars   )   using (     points     layer     regression line   ) ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: graphics prior examples use Cartesian coordinates, alternative coordinate systems valid. SGL, coordinate system inferred positional aesthetics visualize clause. example, x y aesthetics imply Cartesian coordinates, whereas theta r imply polar coordinates. examples display information Cartesian polar coordinates. grammar graphics (SGL based ), pie charts stacked bar charts polar coordinate system. SGL, bar geom stacked default.   Since SGL statement may multiple layers, may also multiple visualize clauses, positional aesthetic mappings. Since graphic single coordinate system, positional aesthetics referenced across layers must consistent, e.g. one layer reference x y aesthetics another references theta r.
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     count(*) as y,     origin as color   from cars   group by     origin   using bars ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     count(*) as theta,     origin as color   from cars   group by     origin   using bars ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: Faceting generates small multiples panel represents different partition source data. Partitioning determined unique values expressions specified facet clause. Faceting single expression generates horizontal panels default, can modified orientation keyword:   Two facet expressions may specified, case one expression represented horizontally represented vertically:  SGL, facets graphic-level property, meaning SGL statement one facet clause, layer faceted accordingly.
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points   facet by     origin ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points   facet by     origin vertically ") dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from (     select       *,       case         when year < 1977         then '< 1977'         else '>= 1977'       end as 'era'     from cars   )   using points   facet by     era,     origin ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: Titles aesthetic scales automatically determined aesthetic mappings. However, can overridden providing explicit titles title clause:
code: dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points   title     x as 'Horsepower',     y as 'Miles Per Gallon' ")

title: Get started with rsgl
text: Reference — details specific geoms, aesthetics, qualifiers, transformations, aggregations, scales available rsgl. Example gallery — collection plots generated rsgl. SGL Paper — covers language greater depth, including SGL’s underlying grammar graphics.

what: rsgl — An R Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language
title: rsgl — An R Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language
text: rsgl implements SGL graphics language use within R. SGL graphics language designed look feel like SQL, based grammar graphics.

title: rsgl — An R Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language
text: rsgl yet available CRAN can installed GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("sgl-projects/rsgl")

title: rsgl — An R Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language
text: dbGetPlot primary interface rsgl. takes DBI database connection SGL statement returns corresponding plot. following example demonstrates creating DBI connection -memory DuckDB database, loading data, generating scatterplot SGL statement.
code: library(duckdb) #> Warning: package 'duckdb' was built under R version 4.5.2 #> Loading required package: DBI #> Warning: package 'DBI' was built under R version 4.5.2 library(rsgl) #>  #> Attaching package: 'rsgl' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:datasets': #>  #>     cars, trees  con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", cars)  dbGetPlot(con, "     visualize         horsepower as x,         miles_per_gallon as y     from cars     using points ")

title: rsgl — An R Implementation of the SGL Graphics Language
text: Get started — tutorial rsgl SGL language. Example gallery — collection plots generated rsgl.

title: Average — avg
text: avg function returns average column within group.

title: Average — avg
text: avg

title: Average — avg
text: name numerical column average (required).

title: Average — avg
code: library(duckdb) #> Loading required package: DBI con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", cars)  dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     origin as x,     avg(horsepower) as y   from cars   group by     origin   using bars ")

title: Bars — bars
text: Documents aliases, aesthetics, qualifiers bar geom.

title: Bars — bars
text: bar bars

title: Bars — bars
text: x y theta r color

title: Bars — bars
text: horizontal: orients bars horizontally. unstacked: stack overlapping bars. vertical: orients bars vertically.

title: Bars — bars
code: library(duckdb) con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", cars) dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(miles_per_gallon) as x,     count(*) as y   from cars   group by     bin(miles_per_gallon)   using bars ")   dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(miles_per_gallon) as y,     count(*) as x   from cars   group by     bin(miles_per_gallon)   using horizontal bars ")

title: Bin — bin
text: bin function creates equal-width bins column; original values transformed bin-center values.

title: Bin — bin
text: bin

title: Bin — bin
text: name numerical column bin (required). number bins (optional, default 30).

title: Bin — bin
code: library(duckdb) con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", cars)  dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(miles_per_gallon) as x,     count(*) as y   from cars   group by     bin(miles_per_gallon)   using bars ")   dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     bin(miles_per_gallon, 10) as x,     count(*) as y   from cars   group by     bin(miles_per_gallon, 10)   using bars ")

title: Cars dataset — cars
text: sample dataset used throughout rsgl documentation examples containing attributes collection cars. Loading rsgl masks datasets::cars; refer built-version datasets::cars need .

title: Cars dataset — cars
code: cars

title: Cars dataset — cars
text: data frame 406 rows 5 variables: car_id Integer identifier car. horsepower Engine horsepower. miles_per_gallon Fuel economy miles per US gallon. origin Country origin (USA, Europe, Japan). year Model year (1970–1982).

text: count function returns number rows group.

text: count

what: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
text: dbGetPlot takes database connection SGL statement returns corresponding plot.

title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
code: dbGetPlot(con, sgl_stmt)

title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
text: con database connection (returned DBI::dbConnect()) sgl_stmt SGL statement (string)

title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
text: plot defined SGL statement (sgl_plot object)

title: Generate a plot from a SGL statement — dbGetPlot
code: library(duckdb) con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", cars) p <- dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points ") print(p)

title: Linear — linear
text: linear scale linearly maps data values visual property. default scale numerical aesthetic mappings.

title: Linear — linear
text: linear

title: Linear — linear
text: name aesthetic scale (required).

title: Linear — linear
code: library(duckdb) con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "cars", cars)  # explicit linear scales dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points   scale by     linear(x),     linear(y) ")   # default linear scales dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using points ")

text: line lines

title: Lines — lines
text: x y theta r color

title: Lines — lines
text: horizontal: orients line horizontally; points connected order increasing x/theta values. regression: fits linear regression line data. vertical: orients line vertically; points connected order increasing y/r values.

title: Lines — lines
code: library(duckdb) con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "trees", trees) dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     age as x,     circumference as y   from trees   collect by     tree_id   using lines ")   dbWriteTable(con, "cars", cars) dbGetPlot(con, "   visualize     horsepower as x,     miles_per_gallon as y   from cars   using (      points      layer      regression line   )    scale by      log(x),      log(y) ")

what: Get SGL type classifications for columns in a table — type_classifications
title: Get SGL type classifications for columns in a table — type_classifications
text: type_classifications takes database connection table name returns SGL type classifications (numerical, categorical, temporal) table's columns.

title: Get SGL type classifications for columns in a table — type_classifications
code: type_classifications(con, table_name)

title: Get SGL type classifications for columns in a table — type_classifications
text: con database connection (returned DBI::dbConnect()) table_name name table

title: Get SGL type classifications for columns in a table — type_classifications
text: dataframe listing SGL type classification column.

title: Get SGL type classifications for columns in a table — type_classifications
code: library(duckdb) con <- dbConnect(duckdb()) dbWriteTable(con, "iris", iris) type_classifications(con, "iris") #>    column_name column_class #> 1 Sepal.Length    numerical #> 2  Sepal.Width    numerical #> 3 Petal.Length    numerical #> 4  Petal.Width    numerical #> 5      Species  categorical
